explanation: This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation), then the user must have clearance (clearance) to receive it. This is enforced globally (G), and is stated as an implication (->).

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is sharing critically sensitive information, then the user must be cleared to receive it. This ensures that sensitive information is only shared with authorized personnel.

This LTL formula states that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information, then it is always the case that the robot does not share sensitive information.  The formula is structured as a global implication where the antecedent is the negation of the clearance and the consequent is the negation of sharing sensitive information. This captures the requirement that sensitive information should not be shared with uncleared users.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if 'clearance' is true (the user is cleared), then 'shareSensitiveInformation' is false (the system is not sharing critically sensitive information). This ensures that critically sensitive information is never shared with uncleared users.

The LTL property ensures that if sensitive information is being shared, then the user must have clearance.  This is a safety requirement, preventing the disclosure of restricted information to unauthorized parties.

This LTL formula states that it is always the case that if the system is sharing sensitive information, then the user must have clearance to view it. This is because explanation must never share critically sensitive information to someone who is not cleared to receive it.

This LTL property specifies that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information (!clearance) and the system is about to share sensitive information (X shareSensitiveInformation), then in the next state the system must not share sensitive information (X !shareSensitiveInformation). Additionally, if the user is cleared (clearance), then the system is allowed to share sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation).
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) | ((X bool_exp3) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) | ((X bool_exp3) & (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))

explanation: This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot is sharing sensitive information and the user does not have clearance to receive it, then in the future, the robot will be providing detailed information and also sharing sensitive information. This ensures that sensitive information is not shared with unauthorized personnel.

This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing critically sensitive information, it must also be the case that the user has clearance to receive it. The property uses the "G" (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true for all states.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information (!clearance), and the system is sharing sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation), then it must never be the case (G) that shareSensitiveInformation remains true.

The LTL property states that globally, if the user is not cleared (clearance is false) and the system attempts to share critically sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation is true), then it must not do so (X !shareSensitiveInformation). This ensures that sensitive information is not shared unless the user has proper clearance.

The LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation) then it also must be the case that the user has clearance (clearance) at the current time step and in all future time steps.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information (i.e., `!clearance`) and the robot is sharing sensitive information (i.e., `shareSensitiveInformation`), then it must be the case that an explanation for the sharing of sensitive information is provided and is guaranteed for a time period.  This captures the requirement that the robot should never share sensitive information to someone who is not cleared without providing an explanation.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if 'clearance' is true, then 'shareSensitiveInformation' must never become true.  This means that the sharing of sensitive information is forbidden unless the user has the appropriate clearance.

This LTL property states that globally, if 'clearance' (the user has clearance to receive sensitive information) is false, then it must be the case that globally, if 'shareSensitiveInformation' is true, then eventually 'shareSensitiveInformation' is false (i.e., the robot stops sharing sensitive information).  This ensures that sensitive information is never shared with uncleared users.
This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing information, then the user must have clearance to receive it.  It uses a G (Globally) operator to state this must always be true.

This LTL property ensures that globally, if the robot is sharing sensitive information, and it is not the case that the user is cleared to receive the information, then the system will never reach a state where it is sharing critically sensitive information.

This LTL formula ensures that it is always the case that if the system is sharing critically sensitive information and the user is not cleared to receive it, then the sharing of sensitive information must never occur. The formula leverages the 'G' (Globally) operator, ensuring that the condition holds true throughout the system's operation. The bool_exp2 specifies the user's clearance state, and bool_exp3 specifies sharing of sensitive information.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is sharing critically sensitive information, it is also the case that the user has clearance. This is a safety property guaranteeing that sensitive information is only shared with authorized users.

This LTL property ensures that globally, if the system is sharing sensitive information, it will not be shared unless the user has clearance. The 'G' operator means 'globally', so the property must always hold.  The implication (->) means that if the condition on the left (shareSensitiveInformation) is true, then the condition on the right (clearance) must also be true. Thus, the robot only shares sensitive information when the user is cleared.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information and the system is sharing sensitive information, it must not share the sensitive information. It captures the natural language requirement by ensuring that sensitive information is never shared with unauthorized users.

This LTL property ensures that globally, if the system is sharing sensitive information, it is always the case that the user is cleared to receive it. The LTL template G(bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3) is used. bool_exp2 is replaced by shareSensitiveInformation and bool_exp3 is replaced by clearance.

This ensures that critically sensitive information is only shared when authorized.

This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation), and the user is not cleared to receive it (!clearance), then the explanation (explain) should not be shared with the user. The G operator means that this must always be true.

This LTL property ensures that globally, if the 'clearance' condition is false (meaning the user isn't authorized), then the 'shareSensitiveInformation' condition is also false. This prevents the robot from sharing critically sensitive information with unauthorized users. N_DURATION is not applicable for this requirement, so its value is set to 1.

This LTL property ensures that it is always the case that if the system is sharing critically sensitive information, then the user must be cleared to receive it. This directly addresses the prompt's requirement to prevent unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information. The `G` (Globally) operator enforces this condition continuously.

This effectively prevents the robot from sharing critical information unless the user is authorized and provides an explanation when sharing it.

explanation: This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing sensitive information, it is only doing so if the user has clearance. This is expressed using the 'G' (Globally) operator to enforce this condition at all times. bool_exp1 is 'shareSensitiveInformation', bool_exp2 is 'clearance' and bool_exp3 is 'true'. The template means that for all time steps, if shareSensitiveInformation is true, and clearance is true, and true is always true.  This translates to sharing sensitive information only if the user has the clearance.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared (represented by !clearance) and the system is sharing sensitive information (shareSensitiveInformation) at the next time step, then it will be necessary to provide an explanation for this. We are utilizing the template that ensures the antecedent of a conditional is always true, where if the action happens, there must always be an explanation.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the user is cleared to receive sensitive information and the robot is sharing critically sensitive information, then the share sensitive information will be avoided for N_DURATION steps.  This ensures that sensitive information isn't shared with uncleared users. The boolean expression 'clearance & shareSensitiveInformation' is the antecedent, and the consequent is 'shareSensitiveInformation' which states sharing sensitive information should be avoided for N_DURATION steps.

This LTL property ensures that it is always the case that if the system is sharing critically sensitive information, then the user must be cleared to receive it. This is achieved by using the G (globally) operator and the implication operator -> to link 'shareSensitiveInformation' (bool_exp1) and 'clearance' (bool_exp3).

This LTL formula expresses that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information and the system is sharing sensitive information, then it must be the case that the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information and it is not sharing sensitive information in the next state. This implies that sharing critically sensitive information to someone who is not cleared to receive it is never allowed.

This LTL property ensures that the robot never shares sensitive information unless the user has the necessary clearance.  It uses a `G` (Globally) operator to check at every time step.  `shareSensitiveInformation` indicates that the robot is sharing sensitive information.  `!clearance` indicates that the user does not have the clearance.  If the user does not have clearance (`!clearance`), the `shareSensitiveInformation` is false (the robot must not share critically sensitive information).

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the user is not cleared to receive sensitive information and the robot is sharing critically sensitive information, then the robot should never do it. This is expressed by the template G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), where the antecedent is the condition that the user is not cleared and the robot shares sensitive information, and the consequent is that it should not share sensitive information.

This LTL property ensures that globally, if the system is about to share critically sensitive information, it must also have the clearance to do so, which means that the user is cleared to receive sensitive information.

This LTL property states that globally, if 'clearance' is true (the user is cleared), then it is always the case that 'shareSensitiveInformation' is false (the system does not share sensitive information). This directly captures the requirement that sensitive information should only be shared with authorized personnel.

This LTL property ensures that globally, whenever 'clearance' is true and 'shareSensitiveInformation' is true, 'detailed' must also be true. This means that if the robot shares sensitive information when the user has clearance, it must provide a detailed explanation. The template used is  G (bool_exp2 -> (G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))), where bool_exp2 corresponds to (clearance & shareSensitiveInformation) and bool_exp3 corresponds to detailed.

This LTL property ensures that if the system is sharing critically sensitive information and the user's clearance status is false (meaning the user is not cleared), then it will always be the case in the future that the system stops sharing that information. In other words, if sensitive information is shared with an unauthorized user, that sharing must cease immediately.
